nameMeanings.name: Bainbridge
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Bainbridge in North Yorkshire, named for the Bain river on which it stands (which is named with Old Norse beinn ‘straight’) + Old English brycg ‘bridge’.
History: A family of this name was very prominent in Princeton, NJ, from the mid 17th century.

nameMeanings.name: Ann
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Variant spelling of Anne . In the U.S. Ann is the more frequent spelling.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: Ann Bainbridge was born about 1370, in Yorkshire, England. She had at least 1 son with Robert Hodgson Lausoun. She died about 1440, in Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 72.

fr.lifeSummary: Ann Bainbridge est née environ 1370, à Everwyck, Angleterre. Elle a eu au moins 1 fils avec Robert Hodgson Lausoun. Elle est décédée environ 1440, à Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 72 ans.

ja.lifeSummary: Ann Bainbridgeは約1370年にイギリスヨークシュアイアで生まれた。彼女はRobert Hodgson Lausounとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼女は約1440年にイギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアで72歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Ann Bainbridge(은)는 대략 1370년 영국 요르크실르에서 태어났다. 그녀는 Robert Hodgson Lausoun(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 대략 1440년 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르에서 72세의 나이로 사망했다.
